signals.headline: Nomination triggers speculation on a "Warsh Fed" era, focusing beyond rates to balance-sheet overhaul and inflation cont
signals.raw: Nomination triggers speculation on a "Warsh Fed" era, focusing beyond rates to balance-sheet overhaul and inflation control (Warsh views "inflation as a choice" from excess spending/printing)[1][2].

signals.headline: Warsh, former Fed Governor (2006-2011), historically hawkish on inflation but recently dovish, urging faster rate cuts (
signals.raw: Warsh, former Fed Governor (2006-2011), historically hawkish on inflation but recently dovish, urging faster rate cuts (e.g., to make mortgages affordable) amid AI productivity gains; markets price in June 2026 cuts unchanged[2][4][5].

signals.raw: South Korean outlets highlight risks to Fed independence, "monetary dominance," and global implications; Warsh also tied to Coupang as director[1][2][3][6].

signals.raw: No confirmation or resolution yet (successor announcement noted as imminent in Feb reports); hawkish past vs. recent dovish shift could shift markets on annual inflation targets and rate paths[3][4][5].
